  "japanese": {
    "IsA": [
      "それはアヴァローキテーシュヴァラを描いたシュリーヴィジャヤ時代の青銅の胴体です。",
      "それは仏教における菩薩です。",
      "それはタイで最も美しく、最も広く認識されているアヴァローキテーシュヴァラの彫刻の一つです。"
    ],
    "HasA": [
      "それはわずかに傾いた人型の姿をしています。",
      "それは丸い顔をしています。",
      "それは下向きの目を持っています。",
      "それは部分的に失われた頭飾りを持っています。",
      "それはプラカムビーズの装飾を持っています。",
      "それはネックレスを持っています。",
      "それはパフラットの上腕装飾を持っています。",
      "それは肩の一つに掛けられたチアンバ布を持っています。",
      "それは布に鹿の頭のヤジュルヴェーダビーズの装飾を持っています。"
    ],
    "HasFeatureOf": [
      "それはインドのグプタ・パーラ後期の芸術の影響を受けたシュリーヴィジャヤ芸術の作品として特徴づけられます。",
      "それはインドのグプタ・パーラ後期の芸術の影響を受けています。",
      "それは慈悲（メッター）に満ちた顔として特徴づけられます。",
      "それは智慧（パンニャ）、慈悲（メッター）、忍耐（カンティ）を示す顔として特徴づけられます。",
      "それは浄化された心を持つ姿として特徴づけられます。",
      "それは最高の智慧を持つ姿として特徴づけられます。",
      "それは優れた忍耐力を持つ姿として特徴づけられます。",
      "それは驚くべき作品として特徴づけられます。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それはアヴァローキテーシュヴァラを描写するために使用されます。",
      "それはブッダダーサの寺院公園複合施設であるスアン・モックの主なシンボルとして使用されます。",
      "それは苦しみ（ドゥッカ）や怒り（クロダ）を経験する際に心をクリアにするのを助けるために使用されます。",
      "それはそれを見ることで顔を穏やかにするために使用されます。"
    ],
    "LocatedIn": [
      "それはタイ南部のスラートターニー県チャイヤ郡にあります。",
      "それはバンコク国立博物館のコレクションの中にあります。",
      "それはバンコクのグランドパレスにあります。",
      "それはワット・プラ・ボロムマタート・チャイアの北東の外壁の近くにあります。",
      "それはプラ・シラ・デーン・サム・オン・ヴィハラの近くにあります。"
    ],
    "BuiltIn": [
      "それはシュリーヴィジャヤ時代に建てられました。"
    ],
    "BuiltBy": [
      "unknown"
    ],
    "OriginatedIn": [
      "それはタイ南部で生まれました。",
      "それはチャイヤで生まれました。",
      "それはシュリーヴィジャヤ王国で生まれました。"
    ],

      "それは現代のタイからインドネシアのジャワ島までの地域で作成されました。"

IsA: It is a Srivijaya-era bronze torso depicting Avalokiteshvara.
It is a Bodhisattava in Buddhism.
It is one of the most beautiful and most widely recognised sculptures of Avalokiteshavara in Thailand.
HasA: It has a humanoid figure slightly inclining.
It has a partially lost headwear.
It has decorations of prakham beads.
It has a cloth chiang ba put on one of the shoulder.
It has a dear-headed yajyopavit beads decoration on the cloth.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by similarities with a sculpture of Agastya from Candi Banon in Central Java, Indonesia.
It is characterized by being a work of Srivijaya art.
It is characterized by influence from post-Gupta-Pala art of India.
It is characterized by a face overwhelmed with compassion mettā.
It is characterized by a face showing paññā, mettā, and khanti.
It is characterized by being a figure with a purified mind.
It is characterized by being a figure with top paññā.
It is characterized by being a figure with excellent patience.
It is characterized by being an amazing piece.
UsedFor: It is used for depicting Avalokiteshvara.
It is used as the main symbol of Buddhadasa's temple-park complex Suan Mokkh.
It is used to help one's mind clear up when experiencing dukkha or krodha.
It is used to make one's face pleasant by looking at it.
LocatedIn: It is located in Chaiya District, Surat Thani Province in southern Thailand.
It is located in a collection of Bangkok National Museum.
It is located in the Grand Palace, Bangkok.
It is located near an outer wall to the northeast of Wat Phra Borommathat Chaiya.
It is located near the Phra Sila Daeng Sam Ong vihāra.
BuiltIn: It was built in the Srivijaya-era.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the Kingdom of Srivijaya.
CreatedBy: It was created by an excellent artist.
It was created by a very good-minded and very calm person who also knew a lot about dharma and was very proficient in sculpting.
CreatedIn: It was created somewhere in Chaiya.
It was created in other towns within the Kingdom of Srivijaya.
It was created in areas spanning from modern-day Thailand to the Java island in Indonesia.
DiscoveredBy: It was discovered by Damrong Rajanubhab.
DiscoveredIn: It was discovered in Chaiya District, Surat Thani Province.
It was discovered at Wat Phra Borommathat Chaiya.
It was discovered at Wat Wiang.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a bronze torso.
It consists of a humanoid figure.
It consists of a cloth chiang ba.
It consists of a dear-headed yajyopavit beads.
PartOf: It is part of a collection of Bangkok National Museum.
It is part of the Srivijaya art.
It is part of the post-Gupta-Pala art of India.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Srivijaya art.
It belongs to the collection of Bangkok National Museum.
KnownFor: It is known for being one of the most beautiful sculptures of Avalokiteshavara in Thailand.
It is known for being one of the most widely recognised sculptures of Avalokiteshavara in Thailand.
It is known for its round face.
It is known for its eyes looking downwards.
It is known for its decorations of prakham beads.
It is known for its necklaces.
It is known for its phahurat upper-arm-wear.
It is known for its compassion mettā.
It is known for its paññā, mettā, and khanti.
It is known for being a symbol of Suan Mokkh.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Avalokiteshvara.
It is associated with Buddhism.
It is associated with Srivijaya art.
It is associated with post-Gupta-Pala art of India.
It is associated with the Wat Sema Mueang inscription.
It is associated with Srivijaya King Dharma Seta.
It is associated with the Bodhisattva Vajrapānī.
It is associated with the Bodhisattva Padmapānī.
It is associated with Buddhadasa.
It is associated with Suan Mokkh.
OccurredIn: It occurred in Chaiya District, Surat Thani Province.
It occurred in the Kingdom of Srivijaya.
It occurred in the Grand Palace, Bangkok.
HeldIn: It was held in the Grand Palace, Bangkok.
It was held in the Bangkok National Museum.
About: It is about Avalokiteshvara.
It is about a Bodhisattava in Buddhism.
It is about Srivijaya art.

It is about the Wat Sema Mueang inscription.
